checks.name: structural-coverage
checks.evidence: T-001 through T-006 collectively cover REQ-001 through REQ-012 and AC-001 through AC-014. Each task defines source issue, risk, workflow, requirement scope, planned files, goal, scope, observable Done When criteria, exclusions, rollback, and blockers.

checks.name: blocker-graph
checks.evidence: The pinned graph contains all six task nodes and is acyclic: T-001 and T-002 precede T-003; T-003 precedes T-004 and T-005; T-004 and T-005 precede T-006. Shared downstream-precheck files are serialized transitively from T-001 through T-003 to T-005.

checks.name: draft-planned-state
checks.evidence: Every task is Approval: Draft and Status: Planned while Task-Review-Status is Pending. No task is executable or completed.

checks.name: required-workflow-match
checks.evidence: T-001 through T-005 use tdd for behavioral, validator, and integration changes; T-006 uses acceptance-first for synchronized release metadata. The selected workflows match task risk and each task names concrete pre/post implementation evidence.

checks.name: planned-path-precision
checks.evidence: Implementation entry points, contracts, integration files, tests, reports, and release surfaces are named with repository-relative paths. T-002's bounded tests/fixtures/workflow-state/ root is paired with enumerated AC-002 and AC-006 invalid cases and exact test entry points, so it does not leave the implementation boundary indeterminate.

checks.name: concern-dependencies-done-observability
checks.evidence: Each task states the motivating concern, risk rationale, dependency boundary, rollback, exclusions, and inspectable completion evidence. High-risk tasks require red-to-green logs, parity or integration commands, changed-file hashes, traceability proof, independent review, and exercised rollback.

checks.name: traceability-synchronization
checks.evidence: Task requirement and AC declarations agree with traceability.md. All requirement rows remain Planned and point to the same task/test targets represented by the task decomposition.

checks.name: task-without-direct-ac
checks.evidence: T-001 has no direct AC ownership, but its REQ-003, REQ-008, REQ-010, and REQ-012 mappings remain traceable to acceptance coverage, it is a prerequisite of T-003, and its Done When section independently requires the original transition reproduction, shell/PowerShell regression suites, rejection cases, red-to-green logs, independent review, hashes, traceability proof, and rollback evidence. The supplied contract does not require every task to own an AC.

checks.name: precheck-separation
checks.evidence: The pinned precheck reports workflow_match_precheck PASS and valid blocker formatting with hashes matching the reviewed requirements, acceptance tests, and tasks. No distinct substantive defect was found that warrants duplicating a precheck-owned issue.
